title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working uniform resource-lifetime wrapper while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of uniform resource-lifetime wrapper while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Not Memory
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 9764. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Not Memory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory. hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Composite Resource Object, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Composite Resource Object precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Composite Resource Object, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6142. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Composite Resource Object, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A structure assignment safely duplicates ownership of every nested handle.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Composite Resource Object
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Composite Resource Object: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 4523. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Composite Resource Object.
steps.why: Retrieving Composite Resource Object through the concrete uniform resource-lifetime wrapper case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Composite Resource Object, keep Composite Resource Object connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.
